raw_response: I agree with the consensus that a condylar fracture leading to anterior disruption of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) should be categorized as a complex fracture (Option C). This type of injury not only disrupts the integrity of the condyle but also affects surrounding ligaments, muscles, and blood supply within the TMJ capsule. Given the multifactorial nature of such injuries—impacting both hard and soft tissues—they indeed meet the criteria for being termed "complex," justifying the selection of Option C. Disagreement could arise if focusing solely on the initial bone fracture rather than comprehending the full scope of secondary damages; however, given the emphasis on comprehensive evaluation involving all relevant anatomical structures, my agreement remains firm. Therefore, my final answer choice stands at **C**. Token count approximately reduced to ~85 words.
